items.title: No Surprises Act claim dispute umpires to face public comment period - BenefitsPRO
items.reason: The article reports on upcoming regulatory activity affecting the No Surprises Act's Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process, a core component of the law's implementation and a key area for revenue-cycle operations.
items.summary: The No Surprises Act's Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process—which resolves out-of-network billing disputes between providers and insurers—will undergo a public comment period regarding potential changes to dispute resolution procedures. This regulatory development directly impacts how healthcare revenue-cycle teams manage and resolve balance-billing claims and IDR submissions. The public comment process represents a formal opportunity to influence the rules governing claim dispute resolution, which is a critical operational function for managing surprise bills and out-of-network reimbursement disputes.

items.title: New lawsuit accuses MultiPlan, insurers of out-of-network pricing "cartel" - Insurance Business
items.reason: This item reports a new lawsuit involving payer entities (MultiPlan and insurers) alleging anticompetitive conduct related to out-of-network pricing, which directly falls under payer litigation and enforcement actions affecting provider reimbursement.
items.summary: A new lawsuit has been filed accusing MultiPlan and multiple insurers of participating in an out-of-network pricing cartel, alleging anticompetitive conduct in how they set and manage out-of-network reimbursement rates. The suit targets pricing practices that affect how providers are compensated for care delivered to patients outside insurance networks. This litigation is significant for revenue-cycle teams because out-of-network pricing disputes directly impact provider payment levels and cash flow, and a successful plaintiffs' case could force changes to how payers calculate out-of-network fee schedules industry-wide.
